title: cylicodaphne accedens
intro: Cylicodaphne accedens, also known as Ceylon boxwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lauraceae. It is a small, evergreen shrub native to Sri Lanka, used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it beneficial for treating various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, Cylicodaphne accedens is used in the form of infusions, decoctions, and ointments, and is also used in traditional Sri Lankan medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and digestive issues.
